Class Sprite

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.variable.AbstractVariableStorage<String>
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.stage.sprite.Sprite
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, PhysicalRenderable2D, Renderable, Renderable2D, Physical2D, VariableStorage<String>
public class Sprite extends AbstractVariableStorage<String> implements Serializable, PhysicalRenderable2D
A general-purpose 2D renderable with Scratch-like API. Holds info like position, rotation, etc. Also has several basic operations.

On a Stage, this is a special case of a 2D renderable, since Stage supports cloning sprites, and thus sprites need special handling from Stage.

Note for Scratchers: This class's functionality is similar to Scratch's sprites. There are some differences, for example:
in Scratch, sprites can access the current backdrop. In this implementation, the backdrop is a property of a Stage, not a Sprite. So, to access the current backdrop, you should use Stage.getBackdropIndex() or Stage.getBackdropName() on a Stage instance.

See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Fields inherited from class AbstractVariableStorage

    variables, variablesView

      public void copyPos(Sprite another)
      Moves to the given sprite's position.

      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given sprite is null

    • goTo

      public void goTo(double x, double y)
      Moves to the given coordinates.
      Parameters:
      x - the new x
      y - the new y

    • glideTo

      public void glideTo(Sprite another, long millis)
      Glides to the given sprite position for the given count of milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      another - the sprite to glide towards. The target position is not changed if this sprite moves while this one is gliding.
      millis - how long to glide in milliseconds
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given sprite is null

    • glide

      public void glide(double targetX, double targetY, long millis)
      Glides at the given position for the given count of milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      targetX - x position to glide towards
      targetY - y position to glide towards
      millis - how long to glide in milliseconds

      Sets the current direction to the given value.

    • getDirection

      public double getDirection()
      Returns the current direction. The minimal value is 0 and the maximal value is 360 (not inclusive).
      Specified by:
      getDirection in interface Physical2D
      Returns:
      the current direction

    • sayFor

      public void sayFor(String text, long millis)
      Creates a text bubble near this sprite with the given text that lasts for the given count of milliseconds. Note: If any other thread executes say/think operation, the previous text bubble is discarded.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to appear in the bubble
      millis - how long the text should last

    • say

      public void say(String text)
      Creates a text bubble near this sprite with the given text that lasts until the next text bubble. Note: If any other thread executes say/think operation, the previous text bubble is discarded.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to appear in the bubble

    • thinkFor

      public void thinkFor(String text, long millis)
      Creates a think cloud near this sprite with the given text that lasts for the given count of milliseconds. Note: If any other thread executes say/think operation, the previous text bubble is discarded.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to appear in the bubble
      millis - how long the text should last

    • think

      public void think(String text)
      Creates a think cloud near this sprite with the given text that lasts until the next text bubble. Note: If any other thread executes say/think operation, the previous text bubble is discarded.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to appear in the bubble

      public BubbleState getBubbleState()
      Returns the current text bubble state.

      the current text bubble state

      @NonNull public @NonNull Assets getAssets()
      Returns this sprite's assets (costumes and sounds).
      Returns:
      this sprite's assets

    • setCostume

      public void setCostume(int index)
      Sets the costume index to the given one.

      Note for Scratchers: Unlike in Scratch, the first costume index value is 0.

      Parameters:
      index - the costume index
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no costume with the given index was found

    • setCostume

      public void setCostume(String name)
      Sets the costume name to the given one.
      Parameters:
      name - the costume name
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the name is null
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no costume with the given name was found

    • incrementCostume

      public void incrementCostume()
      Increments the costume index, wrapping around if necessary.

    • decrementCostume

      public void decrementCostume()
      Decrements the costume index, wrapping around if necessary.

    • changeStretchX

      public void changeStretchX(double percents)
      Changes the X axis stretch by the given count of percents.
      Parameters:
      percents - how much to change the X axis stretch

    • changeStretchY

      public void changeStretchY(double percents)
      Changes the Y axis stretch by the given count of percents.
      Parameters:
      percents - how much to change the Y axis stretch

    • changeSize

      public void changeSize(double percents)
      Changes both X and Y axis stretches by the given count of percents.
      Parameters:
      percents - how much to change the size

    • setStretchX

      public void setStretchX(double newStretch)
      Sets the X axis stretch to the given value.
      Parameters:
      newStretch - the new X axis stretch

    • setStretchY

      public void setStretchY(double newStretch)
      Sets the Y axis stretch to the given value.
      Parameters:
      newStretch - the new Y axis stretch

    • setSize

      public void setSize(double newSize)
      Sets both X and Y stretches to the given value.

      Note for Scratchers: In this sprite implementation, there is no getSize(), since size is stored as two separate properties: X stretch and Y stretch. If you're sure they're always equal, you can use either of these properties to determine the size.

      Parameters:
      newSize - the new size

    • getGraphicEffects

      @NonNull public @NonNull GraphicEffectMap getGraphicEffects()
      Returns this sprite's graphic effect map, i.e. an object storing values of graphic effects. It is mutable, i.e. can be changed.

      Note for Scratchers: This method can be used for both changing effect values, setting them, and clearing, either all effects, or the preferred one. Example usages: 

      sprite.getGraphicEffects().put(GraphicEffect.FADE, 100.0); // Equivalent to "set ghost effect to 100" block
// To convert Scratch's "color" effect value to hue degrees, multiply it by 1.8
sprite.getGraphicEffects().increment(GraphicEffect.HUE_SHIFT, 45.0); // Equivalent to "change color effect by 25" block
sprite.getGraphicEffects().clear(); // Equivalent to "clear graphic effects" block
double pixelationValue = sprite.getGraphicEffects().getDouble(GraphicEffect.PIXELATION); // retrieve the current value without changing
double oldWhirlValue = sprite.getGraphicEffects().removeDouble(GraphicEffect.WHIRL); // sets the value to 0.0, returning the old value
      Specified by:
      getGraphicEffects in interface Renderable
      Returns:
      the graphic effect map

    • setVisible

      public void setVisible(boolean visible)
      Shows this sprite if the given value is true, or hides this sprite otherwise.

      When a sprite is visible, it is rendered on the stage. When it is hidden, it is not rendered, but it still exists and can continue to execute code.

      Note for Scratchers: This method matches the functionality of "show" and "hide" blocks.

      Parameters:
      visible - true to show this sprite, false to hide it.

    • render

      public void render(RenderCanvas canvas)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable2D
      Render this object onto the given canvas. The canvas's size will not be more than values returned by Renderable.getPreferredCanvasSize(RenderingContext). The object must be rendered as if without any transformations applied.
      Specified by:
      render in interface Renderable
      Specified by:
      render in interface Renderable2D
      Parameters:
      canvas - the canvas

    • getPreferredCanvasSize

      public Point2D getPreferredCanvasSize(RenderingContext ctx)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable
      Returns preferred dimensions of the object's render. If a dimension exceeds the canvas's dimension, the latter one is used instead.

      Note: This method should not modify the given context in any way to avoid sudden side effects.

      Specified by:
      getPreferredCanvasSize in interface Renderable
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the preferred dimensions

    • getHitbox

      public Hitbox2D getHitbox()
      Description copied from interface: Physical2D
      Returns this object's collision box, as if without any transformations applied.
      Specified by:
      getHitbox in interface Physical2D
      Returns:
      the collision box

    • isVisible

      public boolean isVisible()
      Returns whether this sprite is visible.

      A visible sprite is rendered on the stage, while a hidden sprite is not. However, a hidden sprite still exists and can continue to execute code.

      Specified by:
      isVisible in interface Renderable
      Returns:
      true if this sprite is visible, false otherwise.

    • getCostumeIndex

      public int getCostumeIndex()
      Returns the current costume index.

      Note for Scratchers: This method matches the functionality of "costume number" block.

      Returns:
      the current costume index

    • getCostumeName

      public String getCostumeName()
      Returns the current costume name.
      Returns:
      the current costume name

    • getStretchX

      public double getStretchX()
      Returns the current X axis stretch.
      Specified by:
      getStretchX in interface Physical2D
      Returns:
      the X axis stretch

    • getStretchY

      public double getStretchY()
      Returns the current Y axis stretch.
      Specified by:
      getStretchY in interface Physical2D
      Returns:
      the Y axis stretch

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name. This sprite's sound effects will be applied.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName, boolean waitForDone)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name. This sprite's sound effects will be applied.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      waitForDone - whether to wait until the sound finished playing. If set to true, the return value is essentially useless.
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName, SoundEffectMap soundEffects)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      soundEffects - the sound effect map to use. The changes made to the given map are reflected on the playing sound.
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName, boolean waitForDone, boolean copySoundEffects)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name. This sprite's sound effects will be applied.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      waitForDone - whether to wait until the sound finished playing. If set to true, the return value is essentially useless.
      copySoundEffects - whether to copy the sound effect map. Set this to true if you do not want this sprite's sound effect map changes to affect the sound.
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName, boolean waitForDone, SoundEffectMap soundEffects)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      waitForDone - whether to wait until the sound finished playing. If set to true, the return value is essentially useless.
      soundEffects - the sound effect map to use. The changes made to the given map are reflected on the playing sound.
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • getSoundEffects

      public SoundEffectMap getSoundEffects()
      Returns this sprite's sound effect map, i.e. an object storing values of sound effects. It is mutable, i.e. can be changed. This is also the default sound effect map for playing sounds.

      Note for Scratchers: This method can be used for both changing effect values, setting them, and clearing, either all effects, or the preferred one. Example usages: 

            sprite.getSoundEffects().put(SoundEffect.VOLUME, 80.0); // Equivalent to "set volume to 80" block
      sprite.getSoundEffects().increment(SoundEffect.PITCH, 25.0); // Equivalent to "change pitch effect by 25" block
      sprite.getSoundEffects().clear(); // Equivalent to "clear sound effects" block
      double panValue = sprite.getSoundEffects().getDouble(SoundEffect.PAN); // retrieve the current value
      Returns:
      the sound effect map

    • createVariables

      protected Map<String, Variable<?>> createVariables()
      Description copied from class: AbstractVariableStorage
      Creates a new Map to store variables in. This method must not have side effects and must solely create a new empty mutable map instance (e.g., using constructor).
      Specified by:
      createVariables in class AbstractVariableStorage<String>
      Returns:
      a new variable map
